summary.en: A living ghost town clinging to Cleopatra Hill, where raw industrial history collides with a modern-day arts colony. The entire town is a single, walkable district with steep streets and stunning views of the Verde Valley.

description.en: A quintessential quirky landmark, this 2,600-ton concrete jail slid 225 feet downhill due to mine blasts and unstable ground, symbolizing Jerome's wild history.
Run by the Jerome Historical Society, this dense, artifact-filled museum captures the grit of the town's copper mining heyday and the lives of the miners.
Housed in a former church, this building is the town's memory bank, holding the stories and deep community history that persists long after the mines closed.
The lifeblood of modern Jerome, wander Main Street to discover independent art galleries, boutiques, and craft shops showcasing the work of local artists.

prompt.en: Capture a photo that emphasizes the steep, vertical nature of Jerome's streets and how the town clings to the hillside.
Find a crumbling foundation or a weathered, rusty surface and take a close-up shot focusing on the texture of history.
Find an opening between buildings and capture the stunning panoramic view of the Verde Valley below.
Photograph an interesting window display from one of the many art galleries or quirky shops.
Find a piece of art, jewelry, or a craft item that incorporates copper, paying homage to the town's mining origins.
Take a photo of a building, alley, or staircase that gives off a 'haunted' or mysterious vibe.
